intro: Dichanthelium huachucae, also known as Huachuca Panic Grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing relief from pain and reducing the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Dichanthelium huachucae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
